user.title: Heat Alerts
user.description:
Get notified when it's dangerously hot.

This tool checks the **WBGT** (Wet Bulb Globe Temperature) at your location — a heat measure that, unlike a weather app's "feels like", accounts for humidity **and** direct sun — and sends an alert to your phone when conditions get risky.

For most people there's nothing to configure: confirm your location and pick a device to receive alerts. Open **Advanced** only if you want to change where the heat reading comes from, tune the work-intensity and shift details, or scope to your region's safety standards — sensible defaults are used otherwise.

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.description:
Optional — the defaults work for most people.

**Weather source** — where the heat reading comes from: a forecast for your coordinates (default), a forecast that follows a person/phone, your own temperature + humidity sensors, or a hardware WBGT sensor.

**Work intensity & shift** — how hard the work is and when it happens; these shape the recommended work/rest limits.

**Country / US state** — restricts guidance to the safety standards that apply where you are, so an unrelated rule doesn't drive your alerts.

data.location_entity: Person or phone to follow (tracked source)

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the API. Verify the URL and that the server is running.
error.missing_location: Latitude and longitude are required for location mode.
error.missing_location_entity: A person or device tracker entity is required for tracked_entity mode.
error.missing_sensors: Temperature and humidity entity IDs are required for ha_sensors mode.
error.missing_wbgt_entity: A WBGT entity ID is required for manual_wbgt mode.
error.invalid_time: Shift times must be in HH:MM format (e.g. 07:00).
error.mqtt_not_available: MQTT integration is not configured. Add the MQTT integration in Home Assistant first.
error.missing_mqtt_topic: An MQTT topic is required when using MQTT workload detection.

abort.owntracks_not_configured: The OwnTracks integration is not set up. Add the OwnTracks integration in Home Assistant first, then reopen this QR code.
abort.cloud_unavailable: Could not determine a reachable webhook URL. Enable Home Assistant Cloud (Nabu Casa) or set an external URL, then try again.

show_qr.title: OwnTracks connection QR code
show_qr.description: Scan this in the OwnTracks app: open OwnTracks → Settings → Scan QR code. It configures OwnTracks to send location to this Home Assistant instance over the OwnTracks webhook — no typing required. Edit the identity fields and resubmit to regenerate the code.

data.trackerid: Tracker ID (2 chars shown on the map)

configure.title: Update settings
configure.description: Location and alert device are up front; everything else is under Advanced.

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.description: Weather/data source, work-intensity and shift details, and which regional safety standards apply.

data.location_entity: Person or phone to follow (tracked source)
